source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A dipole of fixed length $2 R$ has mass $m$ on each end, charge $+Q_{2}$ on one end and $-Q_{2}$ on the other. It is in orbit around a fixed point charge $+Q_{1}$. (The ends of the dipole are constrained to remain in the orbital plane.) Figure 1.57 shows the definitions of the coordinates $r, \theta, lpha$. Figure 1.58 gives the radial distances of the dipole ends from $+Q_{1}$. The dipole is in a circular orbit about $Q_{1}$ with $\dot{r} pprox \ddot{r} pprox \ddot{\theta} pprox 0$ and $lpha \ll 1$. Find the period of small oscillations in the $lpha$ coordinate.
extraction_notes: Question text appears to contain OCR or encoding issues such as "lpha" and "pprox"; preserved as-is.
Image field contains a PIL object string representation rather than a file path or URL.

issues: Question text contains explicit OCR or encoding issues noted in metadata, including malformed tokens such as "lpha" and "pprox".

image_dependency: 题干显式引用 Figure 1.57 和 Figure 1.58，并说明图中给出坐标定义和两端到 +Q1 的径向距离。
输入字段标注 requires_image=true 且 text_dominant=false，表明解题主要依赖图像而非纯文本。
该题涉及偶极子在轨道平面内的几何关系，坐标与距离定义具有明显空间/示意图依赖。
multi_step: 要求求解 alpha 坐标的小振动周期，通常需要先建立几何关系与受力/势能表达，再做小角近似并提取简谐振动周期。
题干同时给出 circular orbit、r 近似常量、theta 二阶导近似为 0、alpha << 1 等多个条件，需要组合使用而非单步读取。
答案为符号型解析式而非直接查表结果，说明存在较强的推导链条。
verifiability: 答案是单一闭式表达式 T = 2π√(...)，结果形式清晰，便于后续做符号等价或量纲检查。
answer_present=true 且 answer_type 为短文本，适合规则化比对。
虽有 OCR/编码问题，但核心目标量明确为小振动周期，仍具备较好的结果核验可行性。

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A dipole of fixed length $2 R$ has mass $m$ on each end, charge $+Q_{2}$ on one end and $-Q_{2}$ on the other. It is in orbit around a fixed point charge $+Q_{1}$. (The ends of the dipole are constrained to remain in the orbital plane.) Figure 1.57 shows the definitions of the coordinates $r, \theta, lpha$. Figure 1.58 gives the radial distances of the dipole ends from $+Q_{1}$. The dipole is in a circular orbit about $Q_{1}$ with $\dot{r} pprox \ddot{r} pprox \ddot{\theta} pprox 0$ and $lpha \ll 1$. Find the period of small oscillations in the $lpha$ coordinate.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: Question text appears to contain OCR or encoding issues such as "lpha" and "pprox"; preserved as-is.
Image field contains a PIL object string representation rather than a file path or URL.
metadata.reasoning_chain:
As $\dot{r} \approx \ddot{r} \approx 0$, $r$ is a constant. Also, with $\ddot{	heta}=0$ and $\alpha \ll 1$ (i.e., $\sin \alpha \approx \alpha$), Eq. (3) becomes
$$
mR\ddot{\alpha}+ rac{Q_{1}Q_{2}}{4\pi\varepsilon_{0}} \cdot  rac{\alpha}{r^{2}} = 0.
$$
This shows that the motion in $\alpha$ is simple harmonic with angular frequency
$$
\omega = \sqrt{ rac{Q_{1}Q_{2}}{4\pi\varepsilon_{0}} \cdot  rac{1}{mRr^{2}}}.
$$
The period of such small oscillations is
$$
T =  rac{2\pi}{\omega} = 2\pi\sqrt{ rac{4\pi\varepsilon_{0}}{Q_{1}Q_{2}} \cdot mRr^{2}}.
$$
